subject: btusb: add Edimax BT-8500 vid/pid for FW loading.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: line 344: a10f4246f | btusb: Edimax BT-8500 | c7577014b74c

impact.effect_if_absent: Edimax BT-8500 Bluetooth adapter (RTL8761BUV) cannot be discovered or used; firmware loading fails

notes:
MiSTer fork commit (2021-09-13, author Sorgelig) predates vanilla by ~11.5 months. Device (0x7392:0xc611) for Edimax BT-8500 / RTL8761BUV added to Realtek device table with BTUSB_REALTEK and BTUSB_WIDEBAND_SPEECH flags. Vanilla equivalent appeared in c7577014b74c5369490715015db096182f1a2a23 (2022-08-31, author Szabolcs Sipos -- confirmed via `git log` in /mnt/source/linux) via upstream Bluetooth maintainer, independently and ~11.5 months AFTER the fork. Patch-provenance.md correctly identifies vanilla coverage (line 344, exact match). No carried patch; not present in linux-patches/. No Main_MiSTer userspace coupling detected. Device is present in vanilla 6.18.38 btusb.c at line 797 with identical device ID and driver flags (byte-identical, confirmed via source-grep in this pass).

sonnet-verified correction: `is_backport_of_vanilla` was incorrectly set to `true` in the original record -- this directly contradicted the record's own notes text ("predates vanilla by ~11 months"). Since the fork's commit came FIRST and the vanilla commit has a different author, this is NOT a backport; it is independent/parallel authorship where vanilla later added equivalent coverage. Corrected to `false`.

sonnet-verified: corrected
